name: Al Udeid Air Base
tagline: A major U.S. forward air base and Central Command hub in Qatar.
_note: True OCONUS (Qatar). REMOTE/UNACCOMPANIED expeditionary tour. Al Udeid (379th Air Expeditionary Wing) is a major US forward base hosting AFCENT/CENTCOM-forward elements. Dependents do NOT relocate here -> unaccompanied/expeditionary pattern: no family housing/OHA/DoDEA/BAH/RentCast/SchoolDigger/rent-buy. Personnel live in on-base quarters. Facts verified from official sources (afcent/DoD, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx).

data.Branch: U.S. Air Force (on a Qatari base)
data.Role: Major U.S. forward air base and Central Command-area hub

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Al Udeid Air Base, home of the 379th Air Expeditionary Wing, is one of the U.S. military's most important forward bases, hosting air operations and Central Command-area headquarters functions in the Gulf.
It's a hot, desert, expeditionary posting in Qatar, an unaccompanied tour focused on the mission, with a large but transient, mission-driven population.

data.subtitle: Al Udeid is an expeditionary forward base, not a family-relocation assignment. Personnel live in on-base quarters, so planning is about the separation and the environment.
intro: It's an unaccompanied tour: dependents do not relocate here, so there's no family housing, OHA, BAH, or DoDEA school in the usual sense.
The planning is for the family back home and adapting to a hot, austere, high-tempo environment with on-base contingency living.

recommendations.advice: Personnel live in on-base quarters; there's no family housing.
Sort out where your family lives during the tour and your finances and communication plan early.
Expect extreme desert heat and a high-tempo, expeditionary setting; lean on morale programs.
Expeditionary/unaccompanied tours have their own pay and allowance rules; verify everything with finance.

sections.title: Access
data: Al Udeid is a secure forward base on a Qatari installation, with strict access control and a heightened security posture. Access is coordinated through your command.
Requirements are specific; coordinate everything through your command before you travel.

sections.title: Housing (On-Base Quarters)
data: Because this is an unaccompanied expeditionary tour, personnel live in on-base quarters; there is no family housing.
Your unit handles your quarters assignment on arrival; confirm current arrangements through your sponsor and the base.

sections.title: Schools
data: Because Al Udeid is an unaccompanied tour, dependents do not relocate here, and there is no DoDEA school.
If you have school-age children, they remain at your designated location during the tour; coordinate schooling there and confirm your specific situation with your command.

data.Care: Primary and urgent care for the assigned population; complex cases are referred/evacuated (e.g., to Landstuhl)

data.Exchange: On-base exchange (BX) for the assigned population
data.Note: Shopping and services are on base; off-base access is limited by security posture

intro: An unaccompanied expeditionary tour has its own checklist. The focus is on-base in-processing and squaring away your family's situation back home.

steps.title: Coordinate travel with your command
steps.text: Travel and reporting are tightly coordinated; confirm your itinerary, gear, and entitlements in advance.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, update DEERS, and get the required ID and logistics cards.

steps.title: Get your quarters set up
steps.text: Coordinate your on-base quarters with your unit; this is your home for the tour.

steps.title: Square away the home front
steps.text: Confirm where your family lives during the tour, sort finances, allotments, powers of attorney, and a communication plan.

steps.title: Learn the security posture
steps.text: Understand force-protection rules and any limits on off-base movement.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Enroll in the TRICARE Overseas Program; complex care is referred or evacuated, so understand the process.

data.q: Can my family come to Al Udeid?
data.a: No. It's a remote, unaccompanied expeditionary tour, so dependents don't relocate here, and there's no family housing or DoDEA school.

data.q: Do I get BAH or OHA?
data.a: This is an unaccompanied tour, so the usual family housing allowances don't apply the same way. Confirm your specific pay and allowances (and any continued BAH for family back home) with finance.
In on-base quarters. The unit handles your assignment when you arrive.
High. It's a major forward operating base in the Gulf; the focus is the mission, with morale programs to support the community.

sections.title: On-Base & Around
sections.note: Off-base access is limited by the security posture; this is area context, not travel guidance.

picks.name: Base MWR & community
picks.blurb: On-base recreation and morale programs anchor the tour.

picks.name: Doha
picks.blurb: Qatar's modern capital (access subject to force-protection rules).

picks.name: Persian Gulf coast
picks.blurb: Qatar's Gulf shoreline and desert landscapes.

picks.name: Regional travel
picks.blurb: Doha is a major airline hub when travel is authorized.
